def: (inf. iv. of حدت Heb. He renewed) s. m. Act of making or producing any thing new, production, invention, innovation.
pron. emphat. This very, &c. = ihi, ihiṅ, yahī, q.v.
more: interj. O! sign of the vocative.
pron. This very: see اه ihi.

def: adj. This very = ihīṅ, yahīṅ, q.q.v.
pron. emphat. = ihī, yahī, q.q.v.

ety: inf. n. iv of وجب 'to be necessary,' &c.
def: s.m. Rendering necessary; (in Logic) affirmation (as opp. to 'privation'); assent; (in Moḥammadan law) the first proposal made by one of the parties in negotiating or concluding a bargain
more: (inf. iv. of وجوب) s. m. 1. Rendering necessary. 2. (in logick) Affirmation (opposite to privation). 3. (in Muhammadan law) The first proposal made by one of the parties in negociating or concluding a bargain.
subs: s.m. Proposal and assent or acceptance (in a negotiation of marriage, &c.).

def: n. m. Proposal and acceptance (in a negociation of marriage). The offer and assent must be declared before witnesses to render the marriage valid.

ety: inf. n. iv of جوب
def: s.f. Answering, replying to ( = jawāb denā); granting a favourable reply; accepting (a petition or prayer); acceptance, approval; compliance, consent, sanction, assent; — action of the bowels, motion, evacuation, stool ( = dast).
more: n. f. Granting a favorable reply; answering (a petition or prayer); consent; admission; acknowledgment; acceptance; assent; approbation; compliance; acquiescence. 2. Sanction; grant; concession. 3. Evacuation; motion; stool (dast) . Khul-kar ijābat nahiṅ hotī .
(inf. iv. of جوب) s. f. Answering (of prayer or of a petition), consent, approbation, compliance.

ety: inf. n. iv of وجد 'to be found'; 'to exist'
def: s.m. Creation, production; invention, contrivance
more: (inf. iv. of وجود) 1. s. m. Invention, contrivance. 2. adj. Invented. ايجادکرنا ījād karnā, To invent, contrive, devise.
subs: v.t. To create, produce, originate, invent, design, discover

def: s.f. = P. ازار izār, q.v.: — ijār-band = izār-band.
more: n. m. H. उ up, जड़ root. S. जिरि to destroy. 1. Demolition; destruction; devastation; ravage; havoc; ruin; waste; desolation. 2. An uninhabited place; a desert; wilderness; a ruin; a desolate country. 1. Naṅgā khaṛā ujāṛ meṅ, "Hai koī kapṛe le?" Prov. Stark naked, in the wild, he cries out "dare any body strip me?" 2. Bā'rah-dūnī ujāṛ' . A boundless desert. ujāṛ sū'rat , E. bhoṅḍī —W. adj. Ugly; ill-featured; unsightly; deformed; bad looking. (Applied to a woman who appears without jewels or ornaments.) ujāṛ mūṅh , E. n. m. An ugly, or unlucky face; an ill-omened countenance. Sir jhāṛ, mūṅh ujāṛ (yā pahāṛ) . Prov. A head of hair like a hedge, a face like an ogre. ujāṛ', ū'jaṛ , adj. Demolished; dilapidated; depopulated; laid waste; spoiled; ruined; deserted; uncultivated; abandoned; desolated.

ety: from اجر 'to recompense'
def: s.m. Letting land on lease, letting a house, &c. on rent or hire; land taken on lease; a house, &c. taken on rent or hire; the giving of usufructs for a compensation; a privilege or an income of a variable amount sold for a fixed sum; lease, farm, contract, rent, hire; right, title, monopoly (arising from a lease, contract, &c.)
more: n. m. آجَرَ compensated. 1. A privilege, or income, sold or let for a fixed sum. 2. Lease; lands taken on a farm or lease. Yeh gāoṅ kis ke ijārah meṅ hai? 3. Farm; contract; monopoly; exclusive right or authority. 1. Kuchh āp kā ijārah nahīṅ . 2. Toṛan āë chārā, aur khet pe ijārā . Prov. Came to forage, claimed the field. 4. A farm or lease of the revenue of a village or district, also of customs, or collection of any description, as of customary fees or allowances.
s. m. 1. Hire, rent, a privilege or income of variable amount sold or let for a fixed sum. 2. A farm.
subs: s.m. Deed of lease, lease; agreement; title-deed
ijār-dār, s.m. Lease-holder, lessee; farmer, tenant; contractor; farmer of land or revenue; one who has purchased the labour of another; holder of a monopoly; proprietor
v.t. To farm, lease, let, give out on contract
v.n. To bind oneself, be responsible (for); to engage; agree
v.t. To take on lease, or hire, or contract, &c., to farm
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِجَارَة (ʔijāra).

def: n. m. 1. A farmer; lessee; tenant; a farmer of land or revenue: one who has purchased the labour of another; the holder of a monopoly. 2. Sole master, or proprietor. Aise tum hī ho kyā Brij ke ijār-dār? Song. Holī. ijā'rah de'nā , v. a. To farm; lease; let; give a contract; let in farm (ṭhekā denā). ijārā kar'nā , v. n. To take upon oneself; to be responsible for; to engage; to bind oneself. Jārā lage, to iskā kartā hūṅ maiṅ ijāra, Jiskā kalejah, yāro, sardī ne hove mārā, Nau dām ke woh mujh se le jāë til ke laḍḍū . Nazīr. If cold you feel, then do I make a bond with you, Whoseever liver, friends, is struck with cold, be't you, For half a farthing you may have a til laḍḍā . (The poet has woven his verses out of a familiar street cry).
more: s. m. A farmer of land or of revenue: one who has purchased the labour of another: the holder of a monopoly.

def: n. m. A lease; a document under which a lease or farm is held.

ety: inf. n. iv of وجز 'to be short, &c.
def: s.m. Abridging, curtailing, epitomizing (a book, &c.); abridgment.
more: (inf. iv. of وجز) s. m. Abridgment, the abridging of a discourse or book.

ety: inf. n. iv of جوز 'To go or pass through or along'
def: s.f. Permission, liberty, leave, authority, sanction; leave to depart, dismissal; authority or liberty to do anything; licence; passport
more: n. f. A. جاز for جوز Passed, or made current, (money), held to be right. 1. Sanction; a carte blanche . 2. Permission; liberty; leave. 3. Dismissal; leave to depart. Kyoṅ Sāhab, ijāzat hai? 4. A license; permit; pass; passport. ijā'zat chāh'nā , v. n. To ask leave or permission to depart.
(inf. iv. of جوز) s. f. Permission, leave, dispensation, sanction, commands, orders. اجازت خواه ijāzat-ḵẖẉāh, adj. Asking permission. اجازت دینا ijāzat denā, To permit.
subs: s.m. One who seeks permission or sanction (for any purpose); applicant; petitioner
v.t. To grant leave or permission, to permit, allow, let; to give (one) leave to depart; to grant, admit, concede, comply with; to authorize, sanction; to license; to empower
s.m. A written document by which a permission is conferred; authority; licence.
ex: وہ تمہیں اس کام کرنے کی اجازت نہیں دیں گے۔
vo tumhẽ is kām karne kī ijāzat nahī̃ dẽ ge.
They will not permit you to do such a thing.
(With your permission) let us be on our way
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِجَازَه (ijāza), borrowed from Arabic إِجَازَة (ʔijāza).

def: v. a. 1. To permit; allow; let; grant. 2. To give leave, or permission. 3. To give leave to depart. 4. To admit; concede; tolerate; suffer; accord; comply with. 5. To empower; warrant; authorize.
Unid. n. m. A petitioner; suppliant; applicant.
License to sell. ijāzat milnā , v. n. To obtain permission or leave.

n. m. A written order or permission; a deed of authorization (Mah.); the anumatī patra of Hindu law, or the written permission of the husband to his wife to adopt a son after his decease. — i-tabnīyāt (Mah.) An authority to adopt a son. — wā'pas le'nā , v. n. To withdraw a license.
n. m. amboh , Cor. of. A. ازدحام Multitude; crowd ( bhīṛ ).

ety: inf. n. iv of جل 'to be great'
def: s.m. Magnifying, exalting, honouring; magnificence, glory, honour.
more: (inf. iv. of جلّ) s. m. The bestowing or acknowledging of greatness; magnificence, glory, honour, respect.

ety: inf. n. iv of جلس 'to sit'
def: s.f. The act of sitting of a court of justice; sitting, sessions; a court
more: n. f. جَلَسَ he sat. The act of sitting of a court of justice; sitting; sessions; a court. ijlās karnā . v. n. To sit (in court); preside. bar-sar-i-ijlās, ijlās meṅ , adv. In court; in the presence of; while sitting in court.
(inf. iv. of جلس) s. f. Seating, causing to sit.
subs: v.n. To sit in court; to preside
In the court (of, -ke); in the presence (of), before.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اجلاس (ijlās).

ety: inf. n. iv of جمع 'To assemble, collect'
def: s.m. Act of assembling; assembly; council; senate; court of justice; crowd; collection; amount; whole; — concurrence, agreement
more: (inf. iv. of جمع) s. m. Act of assembling, an assembly, senate, council, court of justice, a crowd, collection, amount, whole. اجماع امت ijmāﻌ-i-ummat, s. m. General assembly of the people, unanimous consent.
subs: s.m. General assembly or convocation of the people; unanimous consent
s.m. The unanimous authority of the companions of Moḥammad on a point of law.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic إِجْمَاع (ʔijmāʕ).

ety: inf. n. iv of جمل 'To collect'
def: s.m. Summing up; recapitulation; abstract, abridgment, summary, compendium, epitome, synopsis; — joint occupancy or possession.
more: n. m. Ped. جمل many strands of a rope put together, aggregate. 1. Sum and substance; recapitulation; resumè . 2. An abstract; summary; compendium; precis; abridgement; epitome; digest; synopsis. 3. Joint occupancy or possession.
(inf. iv. of جمل) s. m. An abstract, compendious account, synopsis.

def: adj. Gross; abridged; succinct; compendious; brief; undivided, held in common (as an estate).
more: adj. Ped. Gross; compendious; abridged; succinct.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اجمالی (ijmālī), from the Arabic verb أَجْمَلَ (ʔajmala, “to summarize”).

ety: inf. n. iv of جري 'to pass along quickly, to flow'
def: s.m. Causing to circulate, putting in circulation, giving currency to, making current; issuing; putting into force or execution; carrying into effect; issue; execution; performance, practice
more: adj. See. ujār . Dark; gloomy; cheerless, 1. Dulhan ke āne se, merā ujṛā ghar bas gayā . Wom. 2. Ujṛe ghar kā balaiṅḍā . Prov. The ridge pole of a deserted house. (contemptuously applied to a very tall, thin, and awkward person).
(inf. iv. of جري) s. m. Performance. اجرا کرنا ijrā karnā, To perform.
s. m. Causing to flow or to circulate; circulation.
subs: v.t. To issue, put in force, perform, execute, &c.

def: n. m. Ped, جري passed. 1. Giving currency or circulation; putting in execution. 2. Issue; execution; service; enforcement; carrying into effect; performance.

ety: inf. n. viii of جہد 'to strive, labour'
def: s.m. Exerting the faculties to the utmost; exertion, effort, earnest endeavour; diligence; — waging war against infidels.
more: (inf. viii. of جهد) s. m. Exertion, effort.

ety: inf. n. viii of جمع
def: s.m. Act of collecting, assembling or gathering together; agreeing together (in opinion); combining, conspiring, leaguing together (against, -par); assemblage, gathering, collection, congregation, accumulation, afflux; combination, league; (in astron.) conjunction
more: (inf. viii. of جمع) s. m. 1. Act of assembling or gathering together; agreeing together (in opinion), assemblage, collection, accumulation, afflux. 2. Conjunction (in astronomy).
subs: To assemble, &c.; to combine or conspire (against, -par)
adv. Collectively, in a body; unanimously
(-par), To be combined, or in league (against); a conjunction (of stars or planets) to bear (upon), or be adverse (to).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِجْتِمَاع (ijtimāʕ).

ety: inf. n. viii of جنب 'to put aside or away'
def: s.m. Keeping away or aloof (from, -se), turning aside or away (from), avoiding, shunning; refraining (from); abstinence; continence; self-control, self-denial; temperance; moderation; austerity; self-mortification
more: n. m. Ped. جنب Put away. 1. Keeping aloof; steering clear of (a person); turning aside from; avoiding. Āp kyoṅ ham se is qaḍar ijtināb rakhte haiṅ . 2. Self-control; continence; self-denial; abnegation. 3. Abstinence; fasting. 4. Temperance; moderation; sobriety. 5. Austerity; moderation; ascetìcìsm.
(inf. viii. of جنب) s. m. Turning aside from, avoiding; abstinence, continence.
wety: From Arabic اِجْتِنَاب (ijtināb), verbal noun of اِجْتَنَبَ (ijtanaba).

ety: contr. from ايك ek, and used in compn.
def: adj. One
more: adj. Used in Comp. for ek one.
subs: s.m. Either of the dishes of a pair of scales
ik-peṅćā, s.m. An ornament for the head
ik-ṭak, adj. Fixed, staring (look or eyes)
adv. In one place, together, united
adj. & adv. Of one mind, unanimous, unwavering, without wavering
(or rājya), s.m. Universal sway or rule
ik-sāṅ, adj. Even, level, plane; equal; similar, same; resembling ( = yak-sāṅ)
v.t. To make even, level, smooth, plane; to dig and plough

ety: inf. n. of عاقب 'to punish,' &c.; iii of عقب 'to follow after'
def: s.m. Punishment, chastisement; torture.
more: (from عقب Heb. ) s. m. f. An eagle. ¶ iﻌḳāb, s. m. Chastisement.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُقَاب (uqāb), borrowed from Arabic عُقَاب (ʕuqāb).

def: s.f. = ايکادشی ekā-daśī, q.v. s.f. The eleventh day of a lunar fortnight.
more: n. f. S. एकादशी . The eleventh day of the lunar fortnight, on which the Hindūs generally keep fast.

ety: S. एक+धारक, or वार+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Single, of one piece; simple (uncompounded, not complex); univalved (in Bot.)
subs: s.f. Simple Rule of Three (syn. trai-rāsik; arbaʻ mutanāsiba).

def: adj. 1. Single; of one piece; the opposite of complex or compound. 2. ( Bot .). One-valved; simple; having no subordinate parts or distinct ramifications. akahrā badan , adj. Thin; lean; lank (body). akahrā bol ; A. mufrad lafz , n. f. ( Gram .). A simple word, in contradistinction to a compound word. ikahrī lāg ; A. arbā mutnāsibā ; S. traī-rās'ika . The simple rule of three.
s.f. A unit; the unit's place in numeration
subs: The figure in the unit's place, the unit.
wety: From ایک (ek, “one”) + ـَائی (-āī).

def: n. f. A unit.

ety: inf. n. iv of قوم 'to be at a stand'
def: s.f. Resting, staying; abode, residence, dwelling.
more: (inf. iv. of قیامت) s. f. Resting, staying, abode, residence, dwelling.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِقَامَت (iqāmat), from Arabic إِقَامَة (ʔiqāma).

def: = اکائی ikāʼī, q.v. s.f. A unit; the unit's place in numeration
subs: The figure in the unit's place, the unit.

def: adj. Ninety-one.

ety: S. एकनवतिः
def: adj. Ninety-one.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit एकनवति (ekanavati)

def: s.m. The letter इ i

ety: S. एकाशीति
def: adj. Eighty-one.

ety: corr. from the English
def: s.f. An act, enactment, statute.

adv. = اکهٿا ikhaṭṭā, q.v. v.t. To collect, get or bring together, gather together, assemble; to amass, accumulate, store, stock, heap or pile up, stack, group; to call together, convoke, convene; to sum up, add up, total; to concentrate, consolidate
more: ( एक One, and स्थान Place) adj. Collected together, in one place.
subs: v.t. To collect, amass, store up, accumulate, treasure up, &c.
or ho-jānā, v.n. To come together, gather, collect, assemble, congregate, flock, herd, crowd; to be added or summed up
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀏𑀕𑀢𑁆𑀣 (egattha), from Sanskrit ए॒क॒स्थ (ekasthá), from ए॒क (eká, “one”) + स्थ (stha, “standing”)..

ety: S. एकत्रिंशत्
def: adj. Thirty-one.
more: adj. S. एकत्रिंशत् Thirty-one. ik-chhat rāj kar'nā , v. a. To hold universal dominion.

ety: S. एक+वर्ण+क
def: adj. (prov.), Of one kind, unmixed, pure, picked.
more: n. m. H. ek nā not one. Picked; unmixed; pure. Ikaunā gehūṅ .

def: s.m. An eruption on the leg.
more: E n. m. An eruption in the leg.
W. n. m. The cross bar on which the pulley turns.

ety: S. एकपंचाशत्
def: adj. Fifty-one.

ety: inf. n. iv of قبل 'to receive favourably,' &c.
def: s.m. Prosperity, good fortune, auspices, felicity; prestige; — acceptance (of a bond, bill, &c.); admission of a claim, &c.), confession, acknowledgment; acquiescence, assent
more: (inf. iv. of قبل) s. m. Prosperity, good fortune, auspices. اقباله ikbāluhu, His good fortune, &c. اقبالہم iḳbāluhum, Their good fortune, &c.
subs: Confession of judgment; admission of a claim
adj. Prosperous, of good fortune, fortunate, happy, felicitous, lucky, auspicious
His good fortune, &c.; iqbālu-hum, Their good fortune, &c.

def: adj. 1. Prosperous; happy; fortunate ( bhāgwān ); felicitous. 2. Lucky; propitious ( subh ). iqbāl-mandī , n. f. Prosperity; good fortune.
adv. v.s.v. اك ik.
To hold universal dominion.
adj. v.s.v. اك ik.

ety: inf. n. iv of قدم 'to go before or forward'
def: s.m. Sending on before, causing to go before; pushing forward; boldness, intrepidity, audacity, resolution, firmness, spirit; endeavour, effort, diligence, attention
more: (inf. iv. of قدم) s. m. Intrepidity, endeavour, effort, diligence, spirit, resolution, firmness, attention.
subs: (-par), To enter boldly (upon), go boldly forward (in), resolutely undertake; to advance (against).

def: One moment.

ety: S. इक्षु
def: s.f. (& m. in Hindī), Sugar-cane ( = ūkh, q.v.)
n. f. Sugarcane. See اُوکهـ.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀇𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀼 (ikkhu), from Sanskrit इक्षु (ikṣu).

ety: S. एक+स्थितः, rt. स्था
def: v.t. To collect, get or bring together, gather together, assemble; to amass, accumulate, store, stock, heap or pile up, stack, group; to call together, convoke, convene; to sum up, add up, total; to concentrate, consolidate
subs: v.t. To collect, amass, store up, accumulate, treasure up, &c.
or ho-jānā, v.n. To come together, gather, collect, assemble, congregate, flock, herd, crowd; to be added or summed up

r: ikhaṭ'ṭā, ikhaṭ'ṭe, ikhaṭ'ṭī, ikhaṭ'ṭe kā ikhaṭ'ṭā
def: adv. H. ek one, ṭhaur, ṭhāoṅ place. 1. Assembled together; collected in one and the same place. 2. In a lot, lump, bundle, parcel, etc.; en masse ; bodily; in the gross; in the mass; huddled together; in the aggregate; simultaneously. Ikhaṭṭā hī māl mārā . He made a good haul. 3. Much; very much; considerably. ikhaṭ'ṭā kar'nā , v. a. 1. To collect; assemble; bring, draw, add, pack, lump, gather together; sum up; heap up; pile; stock; stack; accumulate; group; muster; enlist. Chaukī-dārā ikhaṭṭā kar lo . 2. To convene; convoke; collocate; call together; send for. Chār panch ikhaṭṭe kar-ke nimṭā do . 3. To add up; total; sum up; cast up ( joṛnā ). 4. To gather together; consolidate; concentrate; bring into a focus. ikhaṭṭā ho'nā , v. n. 1. To assemble; meet; come, or get together; congregate. Sab panch ikhaṭṭe ho gaë . 2. To cluster; flock; herd; crowd; throng. Baṛī bhīṛ ikhaṭṭī ho gaī . 3. To be added up, totalled, summed up.

ety: S. एकसप्तति
def: adj. Seventy-one.
adj. S. एकसप्ततिः Seventy-one.

ety: v.n. iv of خفی 'to become imperceptible'
def: s.m. Rendering invisible; concealment.
more: (inf. iv. of خفي) s. m. Concealment.

ety: inf. n. iv of خلص 'to become clear or pure'
def: s.m. Purity; sincerity; candour; affection; pure friendship, sincere attachment; loyalty, fidelity; intimacy
more: (inf. iv. of خلوص) s. m. Sincerity, affection, friendship, love. اخلاص کرنا یا رکهنا iḵẖlāṣ karnā or rakhnā, To esteem, &c., to be sincere.
subs: To cement an intimacy (with, -se); form a friendship (with)
iḵẖlāṣ karnā (-kā), To entertain regard, esteem or love (for); to regard with genuine esteem; to love, hold dear; to be sincere.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِخْلَاص (ixlās), from Arabic إِخْلَاص (ʔiḵlāṣ).

def: adj. Sincere, true, candid, genuine; friendly; affectionate; intimate: — s.m. A friend.
more: adj. Sincere, friendly, affectionate; a friend.

def: s.f. Sincerity; friendliness; affection, &c. = iḵẖlāṣ, q.v. s.m. Purity; sincerity; candour; affection; pure friendship, sincere attachment; loyalty, fidelity; intimacy
subs: To cement an intimacy (with, -se); form a friendship (with)
iḵẖlāṣ karnā (-kā), To entertain regard, esteem or love (for); to regard with genuine esteem; to love, hold dear; to be sincere.

ety: inf. n. iv of خرج 'to pass or go out or forth'
def: s.m. Expulsion, ejection, dislodgment, turning out of possession, eviction; exclusion; extraction; derivation; disbursement, expenditure, expense.
more: (inf. iv. of خرج) s. m. Drawing forth, derivation, expulsion, evacuation.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِخْرَاج (ʔiḵrāj).

def: s.m. (pl. of the above), Expenses, disbursements, costs, charges.
more: pl. of خراج n. m. Expenses; disbursements ( kharch ); charges; costs. Dohre kharājāt se bȳāh bāī . Mah. Wom.
s. m. plur. (of اخراج) Expenses, disbursement.

ety: inf. n. viii of خفي 'to be or become imperceptible'
def: s.m. Hiding, covering; concealment.
more: (inf. viii. of خفا Heb. He concealed) s. m. Act of hiding, concealing, covering.

ety: inf. n. viii of خلف 'to come after'
def: s.m. Disagreement, difference, variance, discord, dissension, breach, rupture; opposition, contrariety; contradiction; incompatibility, incongruity: — iḵẖtilāf rāʼe. Difference of opinion.
more: (inf. viii. of خلف Heb. He passed, changed, succeeded) s. m. Difference (of opinion), opposition, discord, disagreement, misunderstanding between friends, breach, coolness. (Plur. اختلافات iḵẖtilāfāt ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِخْتِلاف (ixtilāf), from Arabic اِخْتِلَاف (iḵtilāf).

ety: inf. n. viii of خلّ 'to become lean or spare'
def: s.m. Need, want; interruption, disturbance, obstacle.
more: (inf. viii. of خل) s. m. f. 1. Interruption, disturbance, obstacle. 2. Need.

ety: inf. n. viii of خلط 'to mix'
def: s.m. The state of being mixed; mixture; union; amalgamation; intercourse, familiarity, intimacy, friendship, warm attachment (between, -meṅ).
more: (inf. viii. of خلط Ch. He mixed) s. m. Act of mixing, mixture, confusion; intercourse, friendship, conversation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اختلاط (ixtilāt, “mixture”)

ety: inf. n. viii of خرع 'to cut, slit'
def: s.m. Inventing; devising; invention; discovery; introducing
more: (inf. viii. of خرع) s. m. Invention, inventing, fancying. اختراع کرنا iḵẖtirāﻌ karnā, To invent, devise.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِخْتِرَاع (iḵtirāʕ).

ety: inf. n. of عالج 'to treat medically,' iii of علج 'to be strong,' &c.
def: s.m. Medical treatment; a medicine, remedy, cure
more: n. m. علج strove. 1. Cure ( upāë , 1—3). 2. Relief; a prophylactic; a sedative; a salve; an antidote ( dafaiyah , 2). Apne kīye kā kyā ilāj? Prov. ( Khud-kardah rā rāilāje nest . P. Prov.) There is no remedy against one's own act. 3. ( ilāj muāljah ) Medical treatment. 4. Punishment ( sazā ). Bīmār-i-ishq kā jo na tujh se huā ilāj, Kah, aë tabīb! tū hī, ki phir terā kyā ilāj! Zauq. If thou, physician, cant the love-sick cure Say, how thine own sound health canst thou secure? ilāj k. , v. a. 1. To cure ( achchhā k. , 3). 2. To chastise ( ṭhīk banānā ).
(from علج To overcome) s. m. f. A remedy, medicine, cure. علاج کرنا iﻌlāj karnā, To cure, to remedy, to apply a remedy.
subs: adj. Admitting of remedy, remediable; curable
(-kā), To treat medically; to apply a remedy (to), to remedy; to cure; to correct, to chastise
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian علاج ('ilāj), from Arabic عِلَاج (ʕilāj).

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻillat, q.v.), Infirmities; defects; vices, &c.; — causes; pretexts, &c.
s.m. Auction, public sale ( = līlām, nīlām, q.q.v.).
s.m. = īlām, q.v. (These forms appear to be peculiar to Southern India; the words used in the North are nīlām, līlām.)

ety: for A. علاوة, v.n. fr. علو 'to be or become high,' &c.
def: s.m. A small (additional) package or load laid on the top (of a loaded camel or horse); a small load upon a heavier; a superaddition, something added, something over and above; — ʻilāwa, vulg. ʻalāwa, adv. As a superaddition, over and above, in addition (to, -ke); besides, moreover
more: adv. علو superadded. 1. Besides; in addition ( bhī ). 2. Let alone; barring ( bajuz ). alāvah iske, ilāvah bar īṅ , However; notwithstanding that; independent of.
subs: ʻalāwa bar īn, In addition to this; moreover; independently of this; notwithstanding that.

ety: T. ěl or il, 'multitude,' 'crowd'+A g̠ār, 'army,' 'host'; — also written يلغار and ايلغار
def: s.m. Forced march of an army, expedition; raid in force; — mass, crowd, herd; heaps, loads, abundance, a large quantity
more: s. A forced march of an army.
subs: In abundance; in excess, too much.

ety: inf. n. iv of لحد 'to deviate (from)'
def: s.m. Impiety; heresy; apostasy; idolatry; irreligion, unbelief, atheism.
more: (inf. iv. of لحد) s. Irreligion, impiety, heresy, unbelief, apostasy, atheism.

ety: inf. n. iv of لحّ 'to be close' (to), &c.
def: s.f. Solicitation, urgency, importunity, entreaty
more: (inf. iv. of لحّ) s. f. Solicitation, urgency, importunity, importunate entreaty.

ety: inf. n. iv of لحق 'to join, adhere'
def: s.m. Annexation, junction; addition, adjunction; continuity; — fees (formerly) exacted from the parties to a suit.
more: (inf. iv. of لحق) s. m. Annexation, junction, addition, adjunction, continuity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الحاق (ilhāq), from Arabic إِلْحَاق (ʔilḥāq).

ety: inf. n. iv of لہم 'to swallow'
def: s.m. Inspiration, revelation
more: (inf. iv. of لهم) s. m. Inspiration, revelation.
subs: adj. Expressing inspired thoughts, speaking by inspiration.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِلْهَام (ilhām), borrowed from Arabic إِلْهَام (ʔilhām), from أَلْهَمَ (ʔalhama). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi الہام (alham /⁠ilhām⁠/).

def: n. m. From. لـﮩم Inspiring, infusing into the mind. Divine inspiration, or revelation ( ākās-bānī ).

ety: inf. n. iv of لحن 'to intone, chant'
def: s.m. Note, sound, modulation; voice; air, tune; a good voice.
more: (inf. iv. of لحن) s. m. Note, sound, modulation, tune, an air (in musick). ¶ alḥān, s. plur. (of لحن) Notes, tunes.

def: s.m. The hilsā fish, Clupea alosa (see hilsā, and illiś).

ety: S. इल्लिशा
def: s.m. The fish commonly called hilsā, Clupea alosa (much esteemed for the table). See hilsā.

ety: inf. n. iv of لقي 'to meet,' &c.
def: s.m. Throwing down, flinging, casting; imparting, communicating; inspiration.
more: (inf. iv. of لقي) s. m. Inspiration.

def: s.m. A wart. (Syn masā, gūmṛī).
more: n. m. The ace in cards.
adv. If not, if, besides, except, otherwise, else, but, unless.

def: conj. If not, otherwise, besides, except, moreover.

ety: for A. علة, v.n. fr. علّ 'to be or become diseased,' &c.
def: s.f. A disease, sickness, malady, infirmity, weakness; a fault, vice, bad habit; a defect, flaw; a bad or worthless thing; rubbish, filth, dirt; — a cause, an efficient cause; an excuse, a plea; a pretext, pretence; — a charge, count (of an indictment)
more: n. f. عل befel. 1. Cause (باعث 1). 2. A charge ( ilzām , 2); count (of an indictment). 3. A bad habit ( dhat ). 4. Fault; vice; defect; flaw. 5. Rubbish. illat-i-ubnā , Slang, See babes . illat-i-sūrī . Formal or apparent cause, i. e. that form in which its essence consists. illat-i-ٴgāī . Final cause. illat-i-fāilī . Efficient cause. illat lagā lenā , v. n. To be addicted to. illat lagānā, mākhūz k. , v. a. To charge; inculpate; reproach ( ilzām d .). illat-i-māddī . Material cause. illat o mālūl ; S. kāran kārya , Cause and effect ( sabab o musabbab ). ba-illat , adv. Through; for; in a charge of ( ba-sabab ). jurm kī illat meṅ , For any offence. harf-i-illat . The interchangeable letters وا and ي in Arabic.

(from علّ Heb. To attempt, to cause) s. f. 1. Disease, defect. 2. Cause, pretence, pretext. 3. Filth, dirt. (Plur. علل iﻌlal ). حرف علت ḥarf-i-iﻌllat, The interchangeable letter و,ا or ي. Every material substance is said to have four causes: علت صوري iﻌllat-i-ṣūrī, Formal cause, i. e. that form in which its essence consists: علت غاٴي iﻌllat-i-g̠ẖāʼī, Final cause, or purpose for which it was made. علت فاعلي iﻌllat-i-fāʼiﻌlī, Efficient cause (as the maker, if the work of man): علت مادي iﻌllat-i-māddī, Material cause, or the matter of which the thing is made.
subs: Apparent or formal cause (i.e. that form in which its essence consists)
(-kī), To be addicted to the vice or bad habit (of)
(with loc.), To reproach, blame; to charge (with, -kī), to inculpate
Material cause, the matter of which a thing is made
(-ě), or (-kī) ʻillat-meṅ, On account (of), because (of); on the charge (of), for
A weak letter (one of the interchangeable letters ا, و, or ي).

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻillaṭ
def: adj. Having a fault, or vice, or flaw, &c.; of bad habits.

ety: pl. of ʻillī, 'high,' &c., v.n. fr. علو 'to be high'
def: adj. & s.m. High, sublime; — the high, the inhabitants of the higher spheres; — the seventh heaven; the place in the seventh heaven to which the souls of believers ascend, and where the records of men's actions are laid up.

ety: pl. of ʻillī, 'high,' &c., v.n. fr. علو 'to be high'
def: adj. & s.m. High, sublime; — the high, the inhabitants of the higher spheres; — the seventh heaven; the place in the seventh heaven to which the souls of believers ascend, and where the records of men's actions are laid up.

ety: inf. n. of علم 'to know'
def: s.m. Knowledge, learning, science; literature; doctrine
more: n. m. 1. A standard ( jhanḍā , 1). 2. ( alam-shadde ) The spear-headed banner of Hasan and Hosain carried in procession at the Moharram. 3. ( Gram .) A proper noun. 4. ( Geom .) Gnomon. alam-bardār , n. m. A standard-bearer. alam ṭūṭnā , v. n. Mah. Wom. To be scourged by Heaven. Khudā kare tujh pe Hazrat Abbās kāalam ṭūṭe! Wom.

n. m. pl. ulūm . See biddyā , 1 to 4. 1. Knowledge. Ilm-i-shaë beh az jehl-i-shaë . P. Prov. Knowledge is better than ignorance. 2. Science. 3. ( Illit .) Charms. ilm-i-āb ; H. jal-biddyā Hydrostatics. ilm-i-adab ; H. bolī-ras , n. m. Literature. ilm-i-akhlāq ; S. उपदेश विद्या Moral philosophy. ilm-i-ilahī , S. ब्रह्म विद्या Theology. ilm-i-āvāz ; H. shabd biddyā , A coustics. ilm-i-bahas , The art of reasoning; logic. ilm-i-tashrīh ; H. aṅg-biddyā . Anatomy. ilm-i-tavārīkh ; S. इतिहास विद्या Chronology; history. ilm-i-jān , H. jīv-biddyā . Physiology. ilm-i-jamādāt, yā mādanyāt ; H. dhātu biddyā Mineralogy. ilm-i-haivānāt , H. pashu biddyā , The science of zoology. ilm-i-dāṅ, ilm-vālā , adj. Learned; wise. ilm-i-dīn ; S. धर्म शास्त्र Religion. ilm-i-ramal , Cleromancy. ilm-i-tabaī ; H. bastu-biddyā , S. पदार्थ विद्या Natural philosophy. ilm-i-urūz ; S. छन्द शास्त्र Prosody. ilm-i-gaib ;…

s. m. Science, knowledge. علم اسما iﻌlm-i-asmā, The knowledge of the names, i. e. of the attributes of God. علم الـﮩ‍ي iﻌlm-i-ilāh-ī, Theology. علم اليقين iﻌlmu-l-yaḳīn, Certain knowledge, demonstration. علم تصوف iﻌlm-i-taṣauwuf, The mystick, or contemplative science. علم تواريخ iﻌlm-i-tawārīḵẖ, Chronology. علم حکمت iﻌlm-i-ḥikmat, Philosophy, physicks. علم رقم يا غباريا حساب iﻌlm-i-raḳam, or -g̠ẖubār, or -ḥisāb, Arithmetick. علم رياضي iﻌlm-i-riyāẓī, The mathematical sciences. علم سحر iﻌlm-i-siḥr, Magick. علم طبيعي iﻌlm-i-t̤abīʼīﻌ, Natural philosophy. علم عروض iﻌlm-i-aﻌrūẓ, Prosody, versification. علم غيب iﻌlm-i-g̠ẖaib, The knowledge of what is occult or absent, or what has not yet existed, foreknowledge, divination. علم قيافه iﻌlm-i-ḳiyāfa, The science of physiognomy. علم کلام يا بيان يا بلاغت يا معاني iﻌlm-i-kalām, or -bayān, or -balāg̠ẖat, or -maʼāﻌnī, The explanatory science, i. e. Rh…
subs: Literature; ethics, moral science, morality; etiquette
The knowledge of the names (i.e. of the attributes of God)
The art of reasoning, the science of logic
or ʻilm-ě-balāg̠at, 'The explanatory science,' rhetoric ( = ʻilm-ě-kalām)
adj. & s.m. Learned, wise; — a learned or wise man, a scholar
The knowledge of what is occult or absent, or what has not yet existed; the occult sciences; foreknowledge; divination
and ʻilm-ě-qiyāfa, The science of physiognomy
and ʻilm-ě-nishast-o-barḵẖāst, A knowledge of etiquette
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عِلْم (ʕilm).

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻilm
def: adj. Scientific; literary; doctrinal; theoretical.
more: adj. Scientific; literary. ilmī bahs yā mubāhisah , Scientific discussion. ilmī majlis . A scientific or literary society.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِلْمِی (ilmī), borrowed from Arabic عِلْمِيّ (ʕilmiyy), from عِلْم (ʕilm) or ـِيّ (-iyy). By surface analysis, علم (ilm) + ـی (-ī).

ety: for A. علمية, fr. ʻilmī
def: s.f. Learning, scholarship.

ety: inf. n. viii of لأم 'to heal' (a wound)
def: s.m. Healing or closing (of a wound); being allayed or soothed, being cured.
more: (inf. viii. of لأم) s. m. Healing or closing of a wound.

ety: inf. n. viii of لبس 'to cover, clothe'
def: s.m. Being obscure; obscurity, ambiguity, intricacy, perplexity; the circumstance of two words being similar in sound but various in meaning, pun, double-entendre.

ety: inf. n. viii of لفت 'to turn, bend'
def: s.f. Regard, attention, countenance; respect, consideration, courtesy, civility, kindness; (in Rhetoric) An apostrophe
more: n. m. لقت Turning towards another. Regard; attention; respect; courtesy; consideration. iltifāt na kar nā , v. n. Not to attend to; not to treat with consideration; to discountenance; refuse to entertain; reject.

(inf. viii. of لفت Heb. He bent, inclined) s. f. Friendship, kindness, obligation, courtesy, respect, regard, attention; an apostrophe (in rhetorick). التفات کرنا iltifāt karnā, To attend to, to have regard or respect.
subs: (-par, or -kī t̤araf), To attend (to), to countenance; to show regard or consideration (for); to notice
Not to attend (to), not to notice; to discountenance, &c.

ety: inf. n. viii of لہب 'to blaze'
def: s.m. Burning, flaming; heat.
more: (inf. viii. of لہب) s. m. Burning, heat, flaming.

ety: inf. n. viii of لجاء 'To take refuge' (with)
def: s.f. Fleeing to (one) for relief or protection, taking refuge (with); refuge, protection; entreaty, petition, urgent request or prayer, solicitation, supplication
more: n. f. لجا Fleeing for refuge. 1. Importunity; supplication; solicitation. 2. Entreaty ( ardās ); petition; urgent prayer. Dunyā meṅ kab nikaltī hai be-iltijā garaz . Zafar. iltijā kar'nā , v. a. To pray; press; importune; beseech.
(inf. viii. of لجأ) s. f. Fleeing to any one for relief or protection; entreaty, request, petition, refuge, protection.
subs: (-se), To make a request (of), to entreat (of), beseech, pray, importune.

ety: inf. n. viii of لمس 'To touch; to petition'
def: s.m. Prayer, petition, supplication, entreaty, request
more: n. m. لمس Praying. A request; petition.
(inf. viii. of لمس) s. m. f. Beseeching, petitioning, supplication, prayer, request. التماس کرنا iltimās karnā, To beseech, solicit, to request, to petition, supplicate, represent humbly.
subs: (-se), To petition, beseech, entreat, supplicate, request, represent humbly.

ety: inf. n. viii of لوي 'to be bent, crooked,' &c.
def: s m. Hanging back, procrastinating, delaying, postponing, deferring

ety: inf. n. viii of لزم 'To be necessary'
def: s.m. Being necessary or expedient, being assiduous, taking on one's self.
more: (inf. viii. of لزم) s. m. Being necessary or expedient, being assiduous, taking on oneʼs self.

def: prop. n. Jerusalem; — Elias.
n. prop. Elias (said to be a grandson of Shem son of Noah).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِلْيَاس (ʔilyās), from Hebrew אליהו.

ety: inf. n. iv of لزم 'to adhere, stick (to)'
def: s.m. Conviction, confutation; reproof, censure, blame; imputation; crimination; reproach, disgrace, discredit; invective; calumny, libel; (in law) accusation, charge, indictment
more: n. m. لزم Rendering necessary, fastening (a thing) upon one. 1. Censure; blame; imputation; crimination; recrimination; invective; denunciation; libel. 1st speaker. Kachchī kalī kachnār kī, aur toṛat man pachhtāë, Ham ḍareṅ ijlām se, kahīṅ kamar na ṭūṭ jā/?/ë . 2nd speaker. Ām phale ladā phadī, ki ḍālī jhuk jhuk jāë, Tum chaṛho sauk se, ki hai Rām sahāë . Pop. Jogiṛā . 2. In law . Accusation; charge; indictment; impeachment; arraignment; bill of indictment. 3. Reproach; discredit; disgrace; calumny. ilzām dhar'nā , v. a. To impute a fault; find fault with. ilzām de'nā, ilzām lagā'nā , v. a. 1. To impute; taunt, or brand with; denounce. Phr. To lay to one's door; to lay to one's charge; to throw in one's teeth. 2. To accuse; criminate; arraign; indict; to fix a charge; inform against; charge; impeach.
(inf. iv. of لزم) s. m. Conviction, confutation, reproof, censure, blame, proof. الزام پانا ilzām pānā, To be convinced, confuted, &c.
subs: (-par), to impute a fault (to), find fault (with), accuse (of)
v.t. To impute; to denounce; to blame, accuse, indict, charge (with), inform against
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian الزام (ilzām, “requirement, commitment, tie”), from Arabic إِلْزَام (ʔilzām).

ety: S. इमि
def: adv. Thus, so, to such a degree ( = aisā: used chiefly in poetry).

ety: old P. ima; Zend aem; S. idam, ayam
def: adj. This ( = īṅ
subs: The form im occurs only in comp. e.g.)
im-roz, adv. This day, to-day; — imroz-fardā, To-day or to-morrow, soon; procrastination ( = āj-kal, q.v.); īmroz-fardā karnā, To put off; to procrastinate; to shilly-shally

ety: inf. n. iv. of ومٲ 'to make a sign'
def: s.f. Sign, nod, beck, hint, suggestion, indirect reference or allusion; emblem, symptom.
more: (inf. iv. of ومأ) s. f. Sign, nod, wink, hint, beck, emblem, symptom.

ety: v.n. fr. عمد 'to stay, prop,' &c.
def: s.m. A stay, prop, support; confidence, reliance, trust; — a pillar, column ( = ʻamūd)
more: (Heb. from عمد Heb. To support, to stand) s. m. 1. A lofty pillar. 2. Confidence, reliance, trust, support.
subs: 'Pillar of the state'; a proper name.

ety: for A. امالة, inf. n. iv of ميل 'to incline'
def: s.m. Causing to incline, bending; (in Gram.) giving to fatḥa a sound like that of kasra (as when kitāb is pronounced as if written kitīb).

s.m. One who is followed or imitated; exemplar, guide, leader, head, head of a religion (and especially of the Mohammadan religion), patriarch; priest; minister or reader of a mosque; — a large bead in a rosary (which remains fixed in the hand, and is not turned over in counting); — the flag or standard borne in procession before the taʻziya on the first ten days of Moḥarram
more: n. m. Patriarch; prelate; chief; spiritual head (of the Mahomedans).

s. m. 1. A leader in religious affairs, a prelate, patriarch, priest, chief. 2. A large bead in a rosary, which remains fixed in the hand and is not turned over in counting (called by the Hindūs sumeru ).
subs: s.m. (dim. imām-bāṛī, s.f.), The place to which the taʻziya is conveyed and kept in the Moḥarram, and where offerings are made to the dead; a building in which the festival of the Moḥarram is celebrated, and in which services are held in commemoration of the death of ʻAlī and his sons Hasan and Husain; (the same building is sometimes used as a mausoleum for the family of the founder)
s.m. The protecting Imām, one's guardian saint
or paisā, s.m. A piece of money dedicated to Imām zāmin, and fastened on the arm of a person about to leave home, as a protection from evil spirits and the difficulties of the way.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمَام (imām), borrowed from Arabic إِمَام (ʔimām).

def: The protecting imām or imām zāmin kā rupayā, yā paisā . A piece of coin dedicated to Imām Zāmin and fastened on the arm of a person about to leave home, as a protection from evil spirits and the difficulties of the way.

ety: for A. عمامة, v.n. fr. عمّ 'to comprehend,' &c.
def: s.m. The sheet or cloth that one winds upon the head, a turban; a tiara, a crown.
more: n. m. A sheet wrapped round the head as a turban; a tiara.
s. m. A turband, a tiara.

ety: for A. امامة
def: s.f. The office of an Imām.
more: s. f. Office of امام imām.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمَامَت (imāmat), borrowed from Arabic إِمَامة (ʔimāma), from إِمَام (ʔimām) + ـَة (-a).

def: n. m. 1. The place where Mahomedans deposit the tāziah , and where offerings are made to the dead. 2. A building in which the festival of the Moharrum is celebrated, and service held in commemoration of the deaths of ٴAli and his sons Hasan and Husain. Sometimes it is used as a masoleum for the family of the founder.
s. m. The place where the Muhammadans celebrate the Muharram.
s. f. The place where the Muhammadans celebrate the Muharram.

ety: corr. of P. hāwan+dasta
def: s.m. Pestle and mortar.
Illit. n. m. Cor. of P. hāvan-dasta . Pestle and mortar.
adj. Of or relating to an imām.
more: adj. Relating to an imām. imām'yā , n. m. An epithet assumed by the followers of Alī ; a Mahomedan sect; Shiahs, who make Alī the rightful imām immediately after Mohammad.

ety: for A. امامية
def: adj. = imāmī, q.v.: — s.m. An epithet assumed by the followers of 'Alī; the sect of Shīʻas (who hold that 'Alī is the lawful and true imām in immediate succession to Mohammad).
more: (Followers) of the Imām; name of a Muhammadan sect, who make Alī the rightful imām immediately after Muhammad.

ety: inf. n. iv of معن 'to go fast,' &c.
def: s.m. Sharpening, rendering acute or penetrating; acuteness, penetration
subs: s.m. Penetrating glance, attentive or close regard or look.

ety: inf. n. iv of امن 'To be safe or secure'
def: s.m. Belief (particularly in God, and in His word and apostles, &c.); faith, religion, creed; conscience; good faith, trustworthiness, integrity
more: (inf. iv. of امن Heb. Trusted, was secure) s. m. Faith, belief, religion, conscience, credit. ايمان دار īmān-dār, Faithful, conscientious, honest, trusty, believer. ايمان داري īmān-dār-ī, s. f. Faithfulness, conscientiousness, honesty, fidelity, trustiness.
subs: To sell one's conscience; to break one's word, faith, &c.; to sacrifice one's integrity, honour, &c.; to prove faithless or dishonest
adj. Faithful, true, conscientious; — s.m. A believer
adj. (f. -ī), Honest, true, fair, conscientious, trustworthy, trusty
To sacrifice or lose honour, integrity, &c. = īmān bećnā, q.v.
(-par), To believe (in), become a convert (to); to have or put faith (in), repose trust or confidence (in), rely (on); to credit, give credit (to)
īmān-meṅ farq ānā, To have one's honesty or probity shaken or disturbed, to prove faithless, dishonest, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِیمَان (īmān), borrowed from Arabic إِيمَان (ʔīmān), from آمَنَ (ʔāmana). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi ایمان (ayman).

def: s.m. Name of a metre in Hindī versification; name of a musical mode.
corr. for ايمان īmān, q.v.
adj. P. دار keep. Opp. of be-īmān . q. v. Conscientious; honest; true; upright; just; trustworthy; faithful.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ایماندار (imān-dār), from Arabic إيمان (ʔīmān).

def: n. f. Truth; honesty ( īmān , 2).
adj. Of the (Musalman) faith; faithful.

ety: for A. عمارة, inf. n. of عمر 'to stay, abide,' &c.
def: s.f. Building; habitation and cultivation; that by which a place is rendered habitable; a building, structure, edifice; a habitation; a fortification.
more: n. f. عمر lived, inhabited. Any large building; an edifice. Imārat gārat . Prov. House building is ruination. imārat-i-sarkārī , n. f. Public works or buildings. imāratī gaz , n. m. A mason's yard. mahakma-i-imārat-i-sarkārī , G. G. n. m. Public works department.
(from عمر Heb. ) s. f. 1. That by which a place becomes habitable, a publick edifice, a building, structure, cultivation, building. 2. A habitation. 3. A fortification. (Plur. عمارات).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عمارت ('imārat), from Arabic عِمَارَة (ʕimāra).

ety: for A. امارة, v.n. of امر 'to command'
def: s.f. Possession of command; the office, and authority of a commander, governor, &c. (amīr); district under the authority of a governor; a government; command, authority, power, dominion, rule, sovereignty; dignity, grandeur, state, magnificence, pomp, display; increase, abundance, prosperity.
more: s. f. Government, lordship, office or dignity of امیر amīr.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمَارَت (imārat), borrowed from Arabic إِمَارَة (ʔimāra), from أَمَرَ (ʔamara).

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻimārat, q.v.), Buildings, edifices, &c.

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻimārat
def: adj. Of, or relating to, building, &c.
subs: s.m. A mason's yard, or rule.

def: s.m. = S ڐمبها डिम्भा ḍimbhā, s.f. New-born child; young of an animal; infant, child; — blockhead, idiot, fool.

ety: inf. n. vii of بسط 'to spread, expand'
def: s.f. Gladness, joy, delight, cheerfulness, recreation, mirth.
more: (inf. vii. of بسط) s. f. Gladness, joy, delight, cheerfulness, recreation, mirth.

ety: inf. n. iv of مدّ 'to help'
def: s.m. Assisting, aiding, abetting; help, succour ( = madad); donation, gift, endowment.
more: (inf. iv. of مدّ) s. f. Assisting, aiding, succour, help.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِمْدَاد (ʔimdād).

ety: inf. n. iv of مکن 'to hold rank,' &c.
def: s.m. Possibility, practicability; power; contingent existence (in contradistinction to wujūb or necessary existence).
more: (inf. iv. of مکن) s. m. Possibility; contingent existence (in opposition to وجوب Necessity, or necessary existence).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمْکَان (imkān), borrowed from Arabic إِمْكَان (ʔimkān), from أَمْكَنَ (ʔamkana). First attested in 1708 as Middle Hindi امکان (amkan /⁠imkān⁠/).

def: adj. Possible, potential; contingent.

ety: inf. n. iv of ملأ 'to fill'
def: s.m. Filling up, completing; inditing, writing correctly; orthography, dictation
more: (inf. iv. of ملأ) s. m. Filling up, completing; orthography, writing correctly.
subs: imlā-navīs, s.m. One who writes correctly.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمْلَا (imlā), from Arabic إِمْلَاء (ʔimlāʔ).

ety: for A. عملة ʻimlat
def: s.m. Operation, produce of work; a mode or manner of work.
wety: From Arabic عِمْلَة (ʕimla).

ety: inf. n. iv of ملك 'to possess'
def: s.m. lit. 'Putting in possession'; possessions, &c. = amlāk, q.v.
more: s. A freehold property which has reverted to the zamīndār for want of an owner ( Trans. of the Agricultural, &c. Society of India, vol. i. p. 177).

ety: S. अम्लिका
def: s.f. The tamarind tree (Tamarindus indica) and its fruit. (Also written imblī, amblī.)
more: n. f. S. अम्लिका from अम्ल sour. Tamarind; Tamarindus Indica . Wat.
s. m. A tamarind tree. (Tamarindus indica).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀇𑀫𑁆𑀮𑀺𑀕𑀸 (*imligā), from Sanskrit अम्लिका (amlikā). Cognate with Bhojpuri इमली (imᵊlī), Gujarati આમલી (āmalī), Punjabi اِمْلی (īmlī) / ਇਮਲੀ (īmlī).

def: adv. v.s.v. im.
more: (این This, and روز Day) adv. Today. امروز فردا imroz fardā, To-day or tomorrow, i. e. soon. امروز فردا کرنا imroz fardā karnā, To put off, to procrastinate.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمْروز (imrōz).

ety: inf. n. iv of مسك 'to hold, retain'
def: s.m. Keeping back or off, retention; abstaining (from); parsimony; a medicine taken to prolong pleasure in carnal intercourse; scarcity, want
more: (inf. iv. of مسک Heb. and Ch. He held, drew) s. m. Holding, retention; parsimony, abstinence, a medicine taken to prolong pleasure, scarcity, want.

def: adv. v.s.v. im.
more: (for این This, and سال Year) adv. This year.

def: (این This, and شت Night) adv. To-night.

ety: inf. n. viii of محن 'to try, prove'
def: s.m. Trial, test, proof, experiment; examination; inquiry; temptation
more: (inf. viii. of محن) s. m. Proof, trial, examination, experiment, temptation. امتحان کرنا imtiḥān karnā, To prove, experience, try, tempt.
subs: v.n. To undergo or pass an examination
v.t. To examine, test, try, prove; to tempt; to prosecute an inquiry; to make experiment or trial (of, -kā); to check, audit
imtěḥān-meṅ pūrā dar-ānā, To pass successfully through an examination or any ordeal or test.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِمْتِحَان (imtihān), borrowed from Arabic اِمْتِحَان (imtiḥān), from اِمْتَحَنَ (imtaḥana). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi امتحان (amtḥan /⁠imtihān⁠/).

ety: inf. n. viii of مدّ 'to extend,' &c.
def: s.m. Extension, prolongation; protraction; prorogation; — adj. Extended; protracted.
more: (inf. viii. of مدّ) 1. s. m. Protraction, prolongation, extension, prorogation. 2. adj. Protracted.

ety: inf. n. viii of ملاء 'to fill'
def: s.m. Fulness, repletion, surfeit; indigestion.
more: (inf. viii. of ملأ Heb. and Ch. He filled) s. m. Repletion; indigestion.

ety: inf. n. viii of منع 'to forbid, inhibit, &c.
def: s.m. Inhibition, prohibition, prevention, restraint ( = manʻ, q.v.)
more: (inf. viii. of منع) s. m. Prohibition, restraint.

def: ( Mech ) Impenetrability.

ety: inf. n. viii of ميز 'to separate, &c.
def: s.m.f. Separation, distinction, discrimination ( = tamīz); discernment, judgment, discretion; holding oneself aloof; refusal; — good-breeding; ceremony; preëminence
more: (inf. viii. of میز) s. m. 1. Dis crimination, discretion, distinction, separation. 2. Preeminence. 3. Good-breeding, ceremony. امتیاز کرنا imtiyāz karnā, To discriminate, distinguish, discern; to treat with distinction.
subs: (-meṅ), To distinguish (between), to discriminate; to discern; to treat with distinction.
wety: Borrowed from Persian امتیاز

def: adj. Of discrimination; of distinction, distinguished; — of good breeding.
more: adj. 1. Having the quality of discriminating. 2. A person of distinction.

ety: inf. n. viii of مزج 'to mix'
def: s.m. Mingling; being mixed, being blended; being diluted; — mixture, union.

ety: inf. n. iv of مضی 'to pass, go'
def: s.m. Despatching; despatch, transmission.

ety: Zend aēm; S. ayam
def: pron. dem. This; these.
more: A termination of plural nouns with which ne and ko are never used.

def: God willing, if God pleases, please God.
(the formative of the oblique cases plural of the pron. yěh), These; them; this (man) him (by way of politeness)
more: pro. These; they. in dinoṅ , adv. Now-a-days; in the present age.
(a pron. plur. in the oblique case) These, them. ان دنون in dinoṅ, Now a days.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡- (aṇa-), from Sanskrit अन्- (an-), from Proto-Indo-European *n̥- (“not, un-”).

def: s.m. The grape (syn. angūr)
subs: s.m. Fox-grape; black nightshade, Sclanum nigrum.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عِنَب (ʕinab).

ety: for A. انابة, inf. n. iv of نوب 'to return,' &c.
def: s.f., Return to the true faith, conversion to God, penitence, repentance.
more: (inf. iv. of نوب) s. f. Conversion to God, repentance.

ety: inf. n. of عاند 'to act with opposition,' &c., iii of عند 'to decline or deviate,' &c.
def: s.m. Opposition, resistance, disobedience, rebellion; perverseness, obstinacy, stubbornness.
more: (from عند To oppose, to be obstinate or contumacious; analog. to Heb. To twist, to bind) s. m. Obstinacy, perverseness, resistance, stubbornness.

def: intj. Lo! behold! there now!

ety: inf. n. iv of نعم 'to be good; to be agreeable,' &c.
def: s.m. Benefaction, donative, gratuity, largess, favour, gift, present; reward, prize; grant of rent-free land
more: (inf. iv. of نعمت) s. m. Benefaction; a present, a gift, favour, benefit. (Plur. انعامات). انعام اکرام inʼāﻌm-i-ikrām, An honourable gift. ¶ anʼāﻌm, s. plur. (of نعم) Gifts, favours.
subs: s.m. An honourable or distinguishing gift
s.m. pl. Favours and honours; rewards and dignities
s.m. A document conveying an assignment of rent-free land; deed of grant
s.m. Holder of an inʻām or grant of rent-free land
(-ko), To give a reward (to), to reward; to award a prize (to), to distribute rewards or prizes
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْعَام (in'ām, “reward, bonus, gratuity”), borrowed from Arabic إِنْعَام (ʔinʕām), from أَنْعَمَ (ʔanʕama).

ety: for A. انعامة
def: s.f. Benefaction, &c. = inʻām. (Rare.)

ety: v.n. fr. عنّ 'to turn aside,' &c.
def: s.f. A rein; bridle
more: s. f. Reins of a bridle, a bridle.
subs: adv. 'With loose rein'; at full gallop (syn. bag-ṭūṭ, q.v.).

ety: for A. عناية, fr. عني 'to be occupied by or with,' &c.
def: s.f. Favour, bounty; gift, present; countenance, aid, support
more: n. f. 1. Favour (احسان); bounty. 2. Countenance; aid; support. ināyat rakhnā , v. a. To show favor to; look kindly on. ināyat k. , v. a. 1. See ehsān k . 2. To grant; give. inā'yatī , adj. Bestowed; favored.
s. f. Favour, gift, present, bounty. عنايت کرنا iﻌnāyat karnā, To give, to grant.
subs: (-par), To show favour (to), to look with kindness (on)
To show favour (to, -par), &c. ( = iḥsān karnā, q.v.); to present, give, grant (to, -ko), to vouchsafe.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِنایَت (ināyat), borrowed from Arabic عِنَايَة (ʕināya), from عَنَى (ʕanā). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi عنایت ('nayt /⁠'ināyat⁠/).

def: s.f. pl. (of ʻināyat, q.v.), Favours; gifts, presents.
more: s. f. plur. of عنايت q. v.

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻināyat
def: adj. Bestowed; favoured.

ety: from the English
def: s.m. Inch (measure).

ety: see aiṅćnā
def: s.f. Pulling, drawing, attraction; stretching; tightening; tendency; desire; reserve, holding back or aloof; delay, tardiness; scarcity, deficiency, want; — the banking system by which zamīndārs pay the revenue demand
more: n. f. 1. Scarcity; deficiency; drought. Abke pānī kī baṛī eṅch huī . 2. Delay; tardiness; slowness. Tum āne meṅ baṛī eṅch karte ho . 3. The banking system by which zamīṅdars pay the revenue demand. A banyā is called in who pays in the amount and afterwards recovers from each āsāmī the amount payable by him ( īṅch-manautī ).
subs: s.f. Tugging and pulling, scuffling; quarrelling.

ety: see aiṅćnā
def: s.m. Pulling, drawing, attraction.
s.m. = inć, q.v. s.m. Inch (measure).

ety: neut. of aiṅćnā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be pulled, drawn, dragged, or attracted; to be drawn tight, be tightened; to be extracted, drawn out; to be sucked up or in, be absorbed; to hold aloof, keep (oneself) apart; to be drawn, sketched, traced, copied; — to be transferred (as a debt): — iṅć-jānā = iṅćnā.
more: v. n. 1. To be pulled, drawn, dragged, attracted; to gravitate. 2. To be tightened, made or drawn tight or fast. 3. To be sucked in, absorbed, taken in. 4. To be extracted (as matter from a boil). Sārā mavād inch gāyā . 5. To keep aloof; to be stiff or exclusive. Kuchh woh iṅche iṅche rahe, kuchh ham khiṅche khiṅche, Is kash-makash meṅ ṭūṭ gāyā rishtā chāh kā . Arshad. 6. To be traced, sketched, drawn, copied. 7. To be transferred (a debt).

def: prep. At, near, nigh, about; by, close by; with, present with; in the presence of; at the place of; at the time of; among, amongst; at, on, upon; according to (it occurs only in composition)
more: adv. At; near; nigh; before; with; by; about; in; on; according to, etc. ind-ul-istifsār, yā tahqīqāt . During interrogation, inquiry, or investigation. ind-ul-paṛtāī , A. H. On examination or inquiry. ind-ul-tajvīz . Under consideration; pending trial or investigation; before the court. ind-ul-talab , adv. On demand. ind-ul-subūt . On proof; on conviction. ind-ul-zurūrat . When required or demanded. ind-ul-mulāqāt , During the interview. ind-ul-vusūl , On receipt. ind-ul-vuqū . When it happens; in such an event.

(prop. عنديٰ) adv. Near, before, with, about, in, according to, &c. عند البعض iﻌnda-l-baﻌẓ, According to some. عند التحقيق iﻌnda-t-taḥḳiḳ, In truth, certainly. عند الله iﻌnda-l-lāh, With or before God. عند الوصول iﻌnda-l-wuṣūl, On the arrival, when it happens, in such an event. عندالوقت iﻌnda-l-waḳt, In time, at the proper time, in the critical juncture.
subs: On or during interrogation, upon inquiry, or investigation
Under consideration, pending trial or investigation, before the court
At the time of applying a remedy; on the disposing of a case (in a court of justice)
In time of need, when required or demanded, on occasion of necessity
With, or before, God; for God
On the arrival (of); on receipt; when it happens; in such an event
In time, at the proper time; on occasion, in the critical juncture
On the occurrence of the event, when it happens, in such an event.

ety: S. इन्द्र+कारः
def: s.m. A large well of solid masonry, protected by a koṭhī or nād.
more: n. m. S. अन्धु a well. A large well built of solid masonry, protected by a koṭhī or nād .
s. m. A large well of masonry.

def: s. m. Name of a medicinal seed. (Nerium antidysentericum).
s.m. Firewood; fuel (generally); chips; litter, straw, weeds.
s.m. Fuel, &c. = indhan, q.v. s.m. Firewood; fuel (generally); chips; litter, straw, weeds.
more: n. m. S. इन्ध् to kindle, Pālī. edho firewood. 1. Firewood; fuel; wood. Bāp ko āṭā na mile, jo īndhan ko bheje! Prov. May Father get no flour, or I'll be sent to cut wood straight. 2. Litter; orts; weeds; straw.
ex: ہَمیں وِمَان اِینْدھَن اِس ہَوَائِی اَڈّے پہ نَہِیں مِلیگَا.
hamẽ vimān īndhan is havāī aḍḍe pe nahī̃ milegā.
We won't be able to get jet fuel at this airport.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ईन्धन (īndhana).

r: īṅḍhuwā, īṅḍhuʼā, īṅḍwā, īṅḍuwā, īṅḍūʼā, īṅḍvī
ety: S. पिण्ड+उकः
def: A roll, coil, or ring of cloth, straw, or rope, &c, on which a burden is carried on the head, or on which water-jars or other vessels having round bottoms may be supported (syn. guṛarī, jūnā); a cant word for a turban.
more: s. m. A roll or round fold on which a burthen is carried on the head.

ety: inf. n. vii of دفع 'to repel,' &c.
def: s.m. The being repelled or prohibited; repulsion; the being warded off.
more: (inf. vii. of دفع) s. m. The being repelled or prohibited, repulsion.

ety: inf. n. vii of دمل 'to heal,' &c.
def: s.m. Healing (of a wound), cicatrization; recovery, getting well; improvement.
more: (inf. vii. of دمل) s. m. The being healed (of a wound).

def: s.f. The goddess of wealth ( = Lakshmī).

ety: inf. n. vii of درج 'to pass by or away'; 'to ascend,' &c.
def: s.m. Being folded together; being inserted; insertion, entry; — extinction of a family.
more: (inf. vii. of درج) s. m. 1. Being folded together. 2. Extinction of a family.
wety: From Arabic اِنْدِرَاج (indirāj).

r: ʻindīya, ʻindiya, ʻindīyat, ʻindī, ʻind, ī
def: s.m. An assertion of mere opinion of one's own; (one's) peculiar or private opinion
more: n. m. عند within. 1. Opinion; one's own opinion or belief. Inkā bhī indiya le lo! 2. The inner mind; intent. Inkā indiyā hī nahīṅ pāyā jātā .
(from Arab. عندي With me) s. m. Opinion, oneʼs own opinion.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻindiya, q.v.), Assertions of mere opinion; opinions; absurdities, fables, fictions.
s. m. The seat or throne of Indra.
s. f. 1. Name of an insect (a species of acarus of a scarlet colour and like velvet). 2. The wife of Indra.
s.m. The king of the gods, the regent of the visible heavens; the thunderer (he is the Jove of the Hindū mythology); the god of rain
more: s. m. The regent of the visible heavens and of the inferiour divinities.
subs: s.f. The wife of Indra; — name of an insect (a species of acarus of a scarlet colour and like velvet), the red-velvet insect (syn. bīr-bahuṭṭī)
s.m. The ancient name of Dehlī
s.f. The capital of Indra and the abode of the immortals
(indra-jāla), s.m. Deception, cheating; trick, stratagem; magic, juggling; illusion
adj. Unreal, deceptive, illusory; magical; — s.m. Cheat, knave; magician, juggler, conjurer
(indra+yaia), s.m. The seed of a certain medicinal plant, Nerium antidysentericum, sparrow's tongue
s.f. The twelfth day of the month Bhadra, a festival in honour of Indra
s.m. lit. 'The court of Indra'; a place of amusement and pleasure, dancing-saloon, assembly-room
s.m. The abode of Indra, the paradise of the immortals
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اندر (andar, “inside, within”).

def: s.f. The wife of Indra; — the name of a medicine or plant, Vitex negundo.
more: n. f. The wife of Indra. A paragon of beauty. Āj to āp indrānī ban-ke baiṭhī haiṅ. indar kā akhāṛā , n. m. See under akhāṛā .
s. f. 1. The wife of Indra. 2. The name of a medicine or plant. (Vitex negundo).

def: n. prop. Name of ancient Dihlī.
n. prop. The city of Indra.

ety: S. इन्द्र+वारुणी
def: s.f. Wild gourd, colocynth, Cucumis colocynthis; a fruit of beautiful appearance but bitter taste; a beautiful but worthless person: — indrāyan-kā phal = indrāyan.

ety: S. इन्द्र+वारुणी
def: s.f. Wild gourd, colocynth, Cucumis colocynthis; a fruit of beautiful appearance but bitter taste; a beautiful but worthless person: — indrāyan-kā phal = indrāyan.
more: n. f. S. इन्द्रवारुणी 1. Fruit of the coloquintida plant; colocynth; a wild gourd; a fruit of beautiful appearance, but bitter taste. 2. A beautiful but worthless person. indrāyan kā phal . The fruit of Indrāyan; Cucumis colocynthis . Wat. used medicinally.
s. f. Colocynth, wild gourd (Cucumis colocynthis, a fruit of beautiful appearance but bitter taste); hence, a beautiful but worthless person is called اندراین کا پهل The fruit of indrāyan.

def: n. f. 1. A diuretic medicine. 2. Copulation; carnal intercourse. indri ke bas honā , v. n. To be lustful; to feel an uncontrollable desire for carnal intercourse; to be given up to sensual pleasures.
s.f. An organ of sense; the senses (internal or external), the organs of action and perception; the privities, membrum virile; veretrum animalium
more: n. f. S. इन्द्रिय an organ of sense. 1. The five senses, organs of perception. 2. The privities; genital organs.

s. f. An organ of sense, the organs of action and perception (the Hindūs reckoning those of action to be the hand, the foot, the voice, the organ of generation, and the organ of excretion, and those of perception to be the mind, the eye, the ear, the nose, the tongue, and the skin); the privities, membrum virile, veretrum animalium. اندري جلاب indrī-jullāb, A diuretick medicine.
subs: s.m. A diuretic medicine; — carnal intercourse, copulation
To be under the influence of lust, to be lustful; to be given up to sensual pleasure
s.m. The faculty of perception, sense, consciousness.

def: s.f. An organ of sense; the senses (internal or external), the organs of action and perception; the privities, membrum virile; veretrum animalium
subs: s.m. A diuretic medicine; — carnal intercourse, copulation
To be under the influence of lust, to be lustful; to be given up to sensual pleasure
s.m. The faculty of perception, sense, consciousness.

def: s.m. The moon
subs: s.f. A digit of the moon
s.m. The day of the moon, Monday.

def: s.m. Rat; mouse.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اندر (andar, “inside, within”).

def: s.m. Rat; mouse ( = indur).

def: s.m. Rat; mouse.
more: s. m. A rat, a mouse.

def: s.m. = indur, īndur, q.q.v. s.m. Rat; mouse.
more: n. m. S. इन्दूर A rat; mouse; mole.

s.m. = اينڐوا īṅḍwā, q.v. A roll, coil, or ring of cloth, straw, or rope, &c, on which a burden is carried on the head, or on which water-jars or other vessels having round bottoms may be supported (syn. guṛarī, jūnā); a cant word for a turban.
s.f. Dim. of inḍwā, q.v.
more: s. f. A round ornament for the top of the head.

def: Mag.; siraiṅḍā , Gaṛh.; biṛvā , Tir. n. m. H. aiṅḍlī a ring. 1. A roll or round fold on which a burden is carried on the head for supporting a water-jar, etc. 2. A cant word for a turban. W.

r: īṅḍwā, īṅḍuwā, īṅḍūʼā, īṅḍvī, īṅḍhuwā, īṅḍhuʼā
ety: S. पिण्ड+उकः
def: A roll, coil, or ring of cloth, straw, or rope, &c, on which a burden is carried on the head, or on which water-jars or other vessels having round bottoms may be supported (syn. guṛarī, jūnā); a cant word for a turban.
n. f. A small īṅḍwā , q. v. Ex. under abhī .

r: īṅḍvī, īṅḍwā, īṅḍuwā, īṅḍūʼā, īṅḍhuwā, īṅḍhuʼā
ety: S. पिण्ड+उकः
def: A roll, coil, or ring of cloth, straw, or rope, &c, on which a burden is carried on the head, or on which water-jars or other vessels having round bottoms may be supported (syn. guṛarī, jūnā); a cant word for a turban.

ety: inf. n. vii of عکس 'to reverse'
def: s.m. The being reversed or inverted; inversion; reflexion (as from a mirror); repercussion, reverberation.
more: (inf. vii. of عکس) s. m. The being inverted; inversion, reflection, representation of appearances in a mirror.

ety: inf. n. vii of فعل 'to do'
def: s.m. An act which causes a blush (as its effect); shame, modesty; confusion.
more: (inf. vii. of فعل) s. m. Shame, modesty, the being abashed.

ety: inf. n. vii of فكّ 'to separate; set free,' &c.
def: s.m. The becoming separated; dislocation (of a bone); release (from a compact); redemption (of a pledge or mortgage).

ety: inf. n. vii of فصل 'to separate, sift, &c.
def: s.m. Separation, division; adjustment, settlement, decision (of a case, see faiṣala)
more: (inf. vii. of فصل) s. m. Separation, division: settling, decision (of a cause). انفصال کرنا infiṣāl karnā, To decide, to settle.
subs: v.t. To decide, settle ( = faiṣala karnā).
